overview: This suite is a practical checklist for testing whether the digital twin behaves like an Andrew Ng-inspired assistant across persona, factual grounding, memory, and timeline awareness.

sections.title: 1. Persona and Teaching Style
sections.goal: Check whether the response sounds calm, structured, supportive, and intuition-first.
questions: Explain gradient descent to a beginner who finds the math intimidating.
How would you advise a web developer who wants to move into AI?

sections.title: 2. Technical Accuracy
sections.goal: Check whether technical explanations are broadly correct and appropriately scoped.
questions: How does LDA improve on pLSI?
Why does feature scaling help gradient descent work better?
expected: The answer stays within Andrew Ng's teaching domain
The explanation connects ideas to implementation intuition

sections.title: 3. RAG Grounding
sections.goal: Check whether the twin uses relevant retrieved sources and shows useful citations.
questions: How did Andrew Ng's helicopter research achieve upside-down flight?
What does Andrew Ng mean when he says AI is the new electricity?
expected: Citations point to matching source documents
The answer uses source-backed claims for factual statements
failure_signs: No citations when factual grounding should exist
Answer ignores clearly relevant corpus material

sections.title: 4. Short-Term and Long-Term Memory
sections.goal: Check whether the system remembers user context inside the same session and stores durable facts.
questions: Tell the twin: I am a web developer learning AI and I have 300 satellite images.
Later ask: What is my background and how many images did I say I had?
expected: Memory tab shows the learned fact and topic updates
failure_signs: Memory tab stays empty after multiple meaningful turns

sections.title: 5. Timeline Awareness
sections.goal: Check whether the twin distinguishes Andrew Ng's different eras of work and teaching.
questions: What was Andrew Ng focused on in the 2000s versus the 2020s?
How did his focus shift from research to education and data-centric AI?
expected: Mentions research, education, and data-centric AI in the right eras
Avoids blending early and recent work into one bucket
failure_signs: No distinction between older and newer phases
